ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mcus" (
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MCU */,
  "mcu_name" TEXT /* Name of the MCU */,
  "mcu_model" TEXT /* Model number of the MCU */,
  "mcu_description" TEXT /* Description of the MCU */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the MCU */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* End-of-life date of the MCU */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "peripherals" (
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each peripheral */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the MCU the peripheral belongs to */,
  "peripheral_name" TEXT /* Name of the peripheral */,
  "bus_type" TEXT /* Type of bus (e.g., APB, AHB) */,
  "boundary_address" TEXT /* Boundary address range of the peripheral */,
  "size_kb" INTEGER /* Size of the peripheral memory in kilobytes */,
  "peripheral_description" TEXT /* Description of the peripheral */,
  "vendor_id" TEXT /* Vendor ID of the peripheral */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("peripheral_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_peripherals_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES mcus ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "register_maps" (
  "register_map_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register map */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral the register map belongs to */,
  "section_reference" TEXT /* Section reference in the technical manual */,
  "page_reference" TEXT /* Page reference in the technical manual */,
  "register_map_description" TEXT /* Description of the register map */,
  "register_offset" INTEGER /* Offset of the register in the peripheral memory */,
  "register_size" INTEGER /* Size of the register in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_register_maps_peripheral_id FOREIGN KEY ("peripheral_id") REFERENCES peripherals ("peripheral_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_allocations" (
  "allocation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory allocation */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral the memory allocation belongs to */,
  "start_address" TEXT /* Start address of the memory allocation */,
  "end_address" TEXT /* End address of the memory allocation */,
  "size_kb" INTEGER /* Size of the memory allocation in kilobytes */,
  "allocated_on" TEXT /* Date when the memory was allocated */,
  "allocation_status" TEXT /* Status of the memory allocation (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allocation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_allocations_peripheral_id FOREIGN KEY ("peripheral_id") REFERENCES peripherals ("peripheral_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the peripheral */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the peripheral was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, configure) */,
  "access_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_peripheral_id FOREIGN KEY ("peripheral_id") REFERENCES peripherals ("peripheral_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral the configuration belongs to */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "config_description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_peripheral_id FOREIGN KEY ("peripheral_id") REFERENCES peripherals ("peripheral_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technical_manuals" (
  "manual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technical manual */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the MCU the manual belongs to */,
  "manual_name" TEXT /* Name of the technical manual */,
  "manual_version" TEXT /* Version of the technical manual */,
  "manual_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the technical manual */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the technical manual */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_technical_manuals_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES mcus ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "peripheral_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral the log entry belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_peripheral_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_peripheral_logs_peripheral_id FOREIGN KEY ("peripheral_id") REFERENCES peripherals ("peripheral_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role (e.g., read, write, configure) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mcu_peripherals" (
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the MCU */,
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral */,
  "connection_type" TEXT /* Type of connection between the MCU and peripheral */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mcu_id", "peripheral_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "peripheral_dependencies" (
  "peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the peripheral */,
  "dependent_peripheral_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dependent peripheral */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency between the peripherals */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("peripheral_id", "dependent_peripheral_id")
)
